old_exact: a When a the noncompensable organization shall award benefits on an aggravation basis, on the following terms: compensable injury, disease, or other condition, combines with injury
new_exact: When a compensable injury combines with a noncompensable injury, disease, or other condition, the organization shall award benefits on an aggravation basis, on the following terms:
evidence: DB text has severely scrambled word order in statute quotation (paragraph [¶14]). The phrase begins with 'a When a the noncompensable' and the words are transposed. Source PDF shows correct order: 'When a compensable injury combines with a noncompensable injury, disease, or other condition, the organization shall award benefits on an aggravation basis, on the following terms:' This is an OCR/ingestion defect where the statute text was reconstructed in wrong order.
note: Critical fix: statute text is fundamental to the opinion's analysis of N.D.C.C. § 65-05-15 aggravation basis requirements. The scrambled text in DB would confuse readers about the statute's actual language.

Goetz v. Goetz, 2023 ND 53, ¶ 1, 988 N.W.2d 553.
source: PDF source /Users/jerod/refs/nd/opin/pdfs/2023/2023ND120.pdf lines 180-183 (pdftotext output); opinion paragraph [¶14]
evidence: The DB text in paragraph 14 contains a severe text-ordering error: 'did not in make specific circumstances adversely affected the children or resulted in a general decline in the children's condition. Goetz v. Goetz, 2023 ND 53, ¶ 1, 988 N.W.2d 553. findings regarding whether the material change' is corrupted word sequencing. The PDF source clearly shows the correct order: 'did not make specific findings regarding whether the material change in circumstances adversely affected the children or resulted in a general decline in the children's condition. Goetz v. Goetz, 2023 ND 53, ¶ 1, 988 N.W.2d 553.' Confirmed by extracting all words from both versions (after the fix, word counts match exactly at 1706 words and all words appear in the same order).
note: Paragraph [¶14] begins the dissenting opinion. This scrambling is an OCR/ingestion defect. All other paragraphs (¶1-¶13 and ¶15-¶17) have been verified and contain no word-order errors.

evidence: The DB text has irregular line breaks after the citation ('2018 ND 93' on its own line, comma on next line), combined with incorrect verb form 'are' (plural) instead of 'is' (singular). note: [¶22]: The citation should be formatted as a complete unit without intervening line breaks, and the subject-verb agreement requires 'is' not 'are'.
